what_it_does: Records metadata that registers an image attachment in the design-source channel memory, pointing to a specific image URI and noting when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter only; no body content
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Target uses an image:// URI scheme
concerns: No descriptive caption or content describing the image
Target URI cannot be resolved or validated from this file alone
No author, provenance, or versioning fields
notes: A bare metadata stub that registers an image attachment for the design-source channel.
questions: Who added this attachment and why?
Is the target image actually present and accessible?
How does this attachment relate to other design-source records?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is meant to be an attachment record and it functions as one, though it lacks any descriptive content about the image.
intention_signals: It appears to catalog slide/page 13 of a capital-raise image as a retrievable attachment in the design-source channel memory.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed pointer from a channel-memory attachment record to a specific image asset, supporting retrieval and provenance tracking for design-source capital-raise images.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment stub named att-fc966348.md in the design-source channel that registers an images-type attachment pointing to image://design-source/capital-raise/p-13 and records its addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to catalog and make retrievable slide/page 13 of a capital-raise image within the design-source channel memory.
